mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals weakening labor market conditions that suppress consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that lifts corporate revenues and equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during late-cycle expansions indicate capacity constraints and slowing output growth that trigger near-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle periods, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 1.0 percent increases discount rates and raises the cost of capital, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.0, or the signal information coefficient turns negative in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product reflect stronger economic activity that boosts corporate profit expectations and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns, or the signal out-of-sample hit rate falls below 50% across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during post-shock recovery phases signals rapid capacity utilization that lifts earnings forecasts and drives positive equity momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to generate positive 20-day returns during recovery regimes, or the predictive relationship reverses when inflation expectations rise above 3%.
